name: Scholar / Scribe
shortDescription: Literate professional trained in records, texts, and formal learning.
contextNotes: Broad literate profession of archives, schools, courts, and temples.

name: Merchant / Trader
shortDescription: Commercial professional trained in exchange, valuation, and organized trade.
contextNotes: Connects goods, routes, buyers, and records.

name: Courtier / Diplomat
shortDescription: Elite social and political professional trained in ceremony, rank, and influence.
contextNotes: Operates where status, presentation, and political judgment matter.

name: Healer
shortDescription: Caregiver trained in immediate care, treatment, remedies, and later advanced medical branches.
contextNotes: Covers folk, temple, learned, and court healing subtypes.

name: Engineer / Builder
shortDescription: Technical professional trained in design, measurement, and practical works.
contextNotes: Shapes roads, structures, estates, fortifications, and major projects.

name: Sailor / Navigator
shortDescription: Maritime professional trained in crew work, vessel handling, and water travel.
contextNotes: Exists across many coastal and riverine cultures, but becomes more specialized as navigation and trade grow.

name: Herdsman / Rider
shortDescription: Animal-working professional trained in care, movement, and practical use of animals.
contextNotes: Covers pastoral, mounted, transport, and scouting roles.

name: Thief / Infiltrator
shortDescription: Covert operator trained in stealth, entry, concealment, and infiltration.
contextNotes: Covers burglary, spycraft, field stealth, and other covert roles.

name: Performer
shortDescription: Expressive social professional trained in music, movement, narrative, or formal presentation.
contextNotes: Performers exist almost everywhere, but subtype and social role vary strongly by culture, setting, and audience.

name: Soldier
shortDescription: Martial professional trained for armed combat, defense, and battlefield survival.
contextNotes: Broad military family; subtype varies strongly. Real melee training normally includes attack skill plus Dodge and Parry.
